differential_diagnoses: Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System (PACNS)
Leptomeningeal metastases (e.g., primary CNS lymphoma or occult carcinoma)
next_steps: Brain and leptomeningeal biopsy to evaluate for vasculitis, amyloid-beta, or malignancy
Comprehensive CSF analysis including cytology, flow cytometry, oligoclonal bands, IgG index, and infectious workup
MRI venography and high-resolution vessel wall imaging
Systemic evaluation for malignancy (e.g., CT chest/abdomen/pelvis, PET scan)
Consider trial of corticosteroids if biopsy is high-risk or pending

differential_diagnoses: Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System (PACNS)
CLIPPERS (Chronic Lymphocytic Inflammation with Pontine Perivascular Enhancement Responsive to Steroids)
next_steps: Brain and leptomeningeal biopsy of a representative nodular lesion
Brain MRI with high-resolution vessel wall imaging and gadolinium contrast
Serum evaluation for IgG4 levels, ANCA, ACE, and paraneoplastic/autoimmune panels
Full-body PET/CT to evaluate for systemic occult malignancy (specifically melanoma)
Comprehensive CSF analysis including repeated cytology, flow cytometry, and paraneoplastic markers
